conditional_states.condition: Region must remain useful (cached content + offline banner) without connectivity.
Region streams or paginates data and can render before the full set arrives.
The fetch can return 403/authorization failure that must be surfaced distinctly from a generic error.
Revalidation detects the underlying data changed since first render.
Skeleton and refresh indicators must degrade to static under reduced motion.

rationale: Any region that fetches data asynchronously must explicitly model the full async lifecycle. Loading, empty, error, and success are the irreducible quartet; stale is required because cached/revalidated data must signal freshness, and slow-network is required so a slow response shows progress rather than appearing frozen or empty. Collapsing any of these (especially treating error or stale as empty) is the single most common interaction-completeness defect.
evidence: TanStack Query / SWR model fetch as explicit loading | error | success | stale (isFetching/isStale) states.
WCAG 2.2 4.1.3 (Status Messages) requires programmatic announcement of loading and result status.
NN/g response-time research: show progress feedback after ~1s and a determinate cue past ~10s for slow networks.
